markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Persist QueryPlan, RetrievalTrace, candidate sets, selected items, rejected items, and non_hybrid_reason in PostgreSQL/EventLedger. This MT belongs to RetrievalContextAndRanking. Retrieve project knowledge through explainable direct, graph, hybrid, and passage fallback modes.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Retrieval must choose explicit modes: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, or passage_fallback, with non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
Context bundles must be bounded, cited, explainable, restartable, and inspectable through backend and visual-debug surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: RetrievalTraceModel leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: RetrievalTraceModel is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified c5bac6ec; RetrievalContextAndRanking: 46 unit + 10 real-PG tests pass; cheapest-authoritative planner records mode+skip-reason in replayable RetrievalTrace; reuses MT-060 trace/bundle substrate (1 new migration 0260); MT-129 additive QueryRetrievalMode extension; folded-stub bridges (139-142) preserve ProjectBrain/SemanticCatalog/AIReady/ContextPack intent as backend contracts.

evidence.finding: build_plan did not call validate() despite the comment; invariant enforced only at persistence
evidence.fix: build_plan validates A0.5 on construction (debug_assert + tracing error); persistence remains the fail-closed gate

findings.title: build_plan does not call validate() despite the comment claiming it
findings.evidence: planner.rs:345-347 comment says the plan is validated before return, but build_plan (348-369) never calls validate(); the A0.5 invariant is enforced only at persistence (storage/knowledge_retrieval.rs:55 record_retrieval_trace). An in-memory plan can transiently violate the invariant.
findings.fix: Call validate() in build_plan (or remove the misleading comment) so the invariant holds at construction too.

adversarial_review_v2.note: Persistence validates the plan, derives non-empty mode_reason (DB CHECK) + decisions JSON; replay round-trip proven (pipeline_tests:66 decisions[query_plan][plan_id] + retrieval_mode).

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-138 RetrievalTraceModel requires persisting a QueryPlan + RetrievalTrace (with explicit retrieval mode and non_hybrid_reason) into PostgreSQL/EventLedger as a durable, replayable trace. The committed code (storage/knowledge_retrieval.rs + knowledge_retrieval/plan.rs) implements exactly that against the real committed knowledge_retrieval_traces table (migration 0141) with EventLedger receipt linkage, and is proven by real schema-isolated PostgreSQL round-trip + replay tests including negative cases.
findings: storage/knowledge_retrieval.rs:93-162 record_retrieval_trace persists a QueryPlan+RetrievalTrace into the committed knowledge_retrieval_traces row: it calls plan.validate() (line 103, fail-closed on non-hybrid-without-reason A0.5 invariant), derives retrieval_mode via storage_mode_of (one of the five spec strings), non-empty mode_reason from trace.mode_reason(), and full replayable decisions JSONB via trace.to_decisions_json(plan), then appends a KnowledgeRetrievalTraceRecorded EventLedger event and links trace_receipt_event_id. This is the exact MT scope.

knowledge_retrieval/plan.rs:198-202 validate() enforces the spec invariant (retrieval_mode != hybrid_rag MUST carry non_hybrid_reason); plan.rs:338-364 mode_reason() is structurally never-empty (unit-tested mode_reason_is_never_empty at plan.rs:435). Matches DB CHECK on mode_reason. Mode set = none/direct_load/exact_lookup/graph_traversal/hybrid_rag/passage_fallback per constraint.

Real-PG proof is genuine: tests/knowledge_retrieval_replay_tests.rs uses MemoryFixture::setup() -> knowledge_pg() (real schema-isolated PostgresDatabase, pool connect to schema_url, real migrations, real source/span/entity rows in knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs:43-119). Test replay_context_bundle_from_trace_reconstructs_plan_evidence_and_receipts (line 133) round-trips a stored trace back into bundle+plan+evidence+EventLedger receipts and asserts aggregate_type/aggregate_id/run-context; skip_if_no_pg! macro panics (no silent skip). Negative cases at lines 254/296/327 prove fail-closed on missing receipts, aggregate-id mismatch, and missing selected items.

Sub-rule 1 PASS: no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/*Unavailable/deferred-live escape reachable from the MT path. The only 'stub' tokens are descriptive comments preserving folded research-stub intent (e.g. knowledge_retrieval.rs:741, planner.rs:21), not live placeholders.

Sub-rule 2 PASS: every durable resource the MT names (knowledge_retrieval_traces table mig 0141, kernel_event_ledger, knowledge_context_bundles, span/source/passage/entity/claim rows) is touched by real PostgreSQL proof through Handshake-managed PostgresDatabase, not implementer mocks. No SQLite/in-memory anywhere in this surface (grep clean).

Prior adversarial LOW finding (build_plan did not call validate() despite comment) was remediated in commit a46b9e6b; persistence remains the authoritative fail-closed gate at knowledge_retrieval.rs:103. Self-cert sub-rule NA: no ACP lifecycle claimed_by/completed_by transition in play (direct code inspection).

Aside (non-blocking, expected per ground rules): packet/MT spec_anchors cite v02.192 and plan.rs comment cites 2.3.14/v02.178; the unified-surface section numbers resolve in current v02.194. Paperwork-only, not a code defect.
evidence_checked: git show c5bac6ec (impl commit) + git log --grep MT-138 (a46b9e6b hardening)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
